comments: Turn seat facing inward at 45° if external rotation ROM of shoulder is limited. Consequently placing the upper arm in this plane limits internal rotation ROM of shoulder and Supraspinatus will assist slightly in interal rotation.

title: Broom Stick Subscapularis Stretch
preparation: Grasp end of pole with one hand. Flip pole over upper arm. Position bent arm to side of body with other end of pole behind arm. Reach across body as far as possible with opposite arm and grasp lower end of pole. Position elbow at height of shoulder with pole positioned on back side of upper arm.
execution: Pull lower end of pole forward and upward so shoulder is externally rotated. Hold stretch. Repeat with opposite arm.
comments: Before pulling pole forward and upward, attempt to orientate pole as vertical as possible and elbow of upper arm as close to lateral of shoulder as possible.

title: Doorway Subscapularis Stretch
comments: Bent knees slightly so stretch is not limited by hamstring        flexibility and to keep shoulder from being pushed forward.

title: Machine-assisted Chest Dip
preparation: Mount wide dip bar with oblique grip (bar diagonal under palm), arms straight with shoulders above hands. Step down onto assistance lever with hips and knees bent.
execution: Lower body by bending arms, allowing elbows to flare out to sides. When slight stretch is felt in chest or shoulders, push body up until arms are straight. Repeat.
comments: See mount and dismount. Some assisted machines require you to kneel on platform instead of standing on bar. Also see Assisted Triceps Dip.

title: Machine-assisted Chest Dip (kneeling)
preparation: Mount wide dip bar with oblique grip (bar diagonal under palm), arms straight with shoulders above hands. Kneel on padded platform, lowering it down slightly so hips are slightly bent.
execution: Lower body by bending arms, allowing elbows to flare out to sides. When slight stretch is felt in chest or shoulders push body up until arms are straight. Repeat.
comments: See mount and dismount. Some assisted machines require you to standing on bar. Also see Assisted Triceps Dip.

title: Barbell Bench Press
preparation: Lie supine on bench. Dismount barbell from rack over upper chest using wide oblique overhand grip.
execution: Lower weight to chest. Press bar upward until arms are extended. Repeat.
comments: Range of motion will be compromised if grip is too wide.

title: Buffalo Bar Bench Press
preparation: With middle of bar arching upward, lie supine on bench. Dismount barbell from rack over upper chest using wide oblique overhand grip.
execution: Lower weight to chest. Press bar upward until arms are extended. Repeat.
comments: Buffalo bar offers slightly greater range of motion compared to Barbell Bench Press. Range of motion will be compromised if grip is too wide. Also see Bench Press Analysis.

title: Cambered Barbell Bench Press
preparation: With camber arching upward, lie supine on bench. Dismount barbell from rack over upper chest using wide oblique overhand grip.
execution: Lower weight toward lower chest until slight stretch is felt in shoulders or chest. Press bar upward until arms are extended. Repeat.
comments: Cambered bar offers greater range of motion compared to Barbell Bench Press. Range of motion will be compromised if grip is too wide. Also see Bench Press Analysis.

title: Deficit Snatch
preparation: Stand on shallow platform positioned under barbell with balls of feet under bar, hip width or slightly wider than hip width apart. Squat down and grip bar with very wide overhand grip. Position shoulders over bar with back arched tightly. Arms are straight with elbows pointed along bar.
execution: Pull bar up off floor by extending hips and knees. As bar reaches knees, back stays arched and maintains same angle to floor as in starting position. When barbell passes knees, vigorously raise shoulders while keeping bar as close to legs as possible. When bar passes upper thighs, allow it to contact thighs. Jump upward extending body. Shrug shoulders and pull barbell upward with arms, allowing elbows to pull up to sides, keeping them over bar as long as possible. Aggressively pull body under bar. Catch bar at arm's length while moving into squat position. As soon as barbell is caught on locked out arms in squat position, squat up into standing position with barbell over head.
comments: The snatch is one coordinated, continuous movement executed with speed. The grip should be wide enough to allow for full squat with bar at arms length over head. Hook grip is used by advanced lifters to maintain grip during snatch. Do not jerk weight from floor; arise steadily then accelerate. The lift is complete when bar is under control over head. Also see:

title: Block Snatch Pull
preparation: Stand with barbell on blocks with very wide overhand grip. Bend knees and hips so barbell touches thigh, shoulders over bar with back arched. Arms are straight with elbows pointed along bar.
execution: Jump upward extending body. Shrug shoulders and pull barbell upward with arms, allowing elbows to pull up to sides.
comments: Before each pull, make certain back is arched, thighs are close to bar, shoulders are over bar, and arms are straight with elbows pointed along bar. Pull bar up in straight path, keeping bar close to body.

title: Block Snatch
preparation: Stand over barbell on blocks with balls of feet positioned under bar hip width or slightly wider than hip width apart. Squat down and grip bar with very wide overhand grip. Position shoulders over bar with back arched tightly. Arms are straight with elbows pointed along bar.
execution: Pull bar up off blocks by extending hips and knees while keeping back arched. When barbell passes knees, vigorously raise shoulders while keeping bar as close to legs as possible. When bar passes upper thighs, allow it to contact thighs. Jump upward extending body. Shrug shoulders and pull barbell upward with arms, allowing elbows to pull up to sides, keeping them over bar as long as possible. Aggressively pull body under bar. Catch bar at arm's length while moving into squat position. As soon as barbell is caught on locked out arms in squat position, squat up into standing position with barbell over head.
comments: The snatch is one coordinated, continuous movement executed with speed. The grip should be wide enough to allow for full squat with bar at arms length over head. Hook grip is used by advanced lifters to maintain grip during snatch. Do not jerk weight from floor; arise steadily then accelerate. The lift is complete when bar is under control over head. also known as Squat Snatch. Also see:

title: Block Power Snatch
preparation: Stand over barbell on blocks with balls of feet positioned under bar hip width or slightly wider than hip width apart. Squat down and grip bar with very wide overhand grip. Position shoulders over bar with back arched tightly. Arms are straight with elbows pointed along bar.
execution: Pull bar up off blocks by extending hips and knees while keeping back arched. When barbell passes knees, vigorously raise shoulders while keeping bar as close to legs as possible. When bar passes upper thighs, allow it to contact thighs. Jump upward extending body. Shrug shoulders and pull barbell upward with arms, allowing elbows to pull up to sides, keeping them over bar as long as possible. Aggressively pull body under bar. Catch bar at arm's length before knees bend lower than 90°. As soon as barbell is caught on locked out arms in partial squat position, stand up with barbell over head immediately so thighs ride no lower than parallel to floor.
comments: The Power Snatch is one coordinated, continuous movement executed with speed. The grip should be wide enough to allow for partial squat with bar at arms length over head. Hook grip is used by advanced lifters to maintain grip during snatch. Do not jerk weight from floor; arise steadily then accelerate. The lift is complete when bar is under control over head. Also see:

title: Barbell Bulgarian Squat
preparation: Stand facing rack with barbell at upper chest height and short platform behind. Position bar on back of shoulders and grasp barbell to sides. Dismount bar from rack and step back to platform. Extend leg back and place forefoot on platform.
execution: Squat down by bending hip and knee of forward leg. Allow forward knee to bend forward slightly while pointing same direction as foot. Descend with back upright until forward thigh is just past parallel to floor while keeping hip and knee of rear leg as straight as possible. Extend hip and knee until forward leg is straight and repeat. Continue with opposite leg.
comments: Bulgarian Split Squat is widely credited to Ángel Spassov, strength coach of Bulgarian National Weightlifting Team for six separate Olympic games from 1968-1996. Although exercise itself predates Spassov in form, he is recognized for giving it its modern popularity.

title: High Block Snatch
preparation: Stand with barbell on high blocks with very wide overhand grip. Bend knees and hips so barbell touches thighs, shoulders over bar with back arched. Arms are straight with elbows pointed along bar.
execution: Jump upward extending body. Shrug shoulders and pull barbell upward with arms, allowing elbows to pull up to sides, keeping them over bar as long as possible. Aggressively pull body under bar. Catch bar at arm's length while moving into squat position. As soon as barbell is caught on locked out arms in squat position, squat up into standing position with barbell overhead.
comments: The Snatch is one coordinated, continuous movement executed with speed. The grip should be wide enough to allow for full squat with bar at arms length overhead. Hook grip is used by advanced lifters to maintain grip during snatch. Also see:

title: High Block Power Snatch
preparation: Stand with barbell on high blocks with very wide overhand grip. Bend knees and hips so barbell touches thighs, shoulders over bar with back arched. Arms are straight with elbows pointed along bar.
execution: Jump upward extending body. Shrug shoulders and pull barbell upward with arms, allowing elbows to pull up to sides, keeping them over bar as long as possible. Aggressively pull body under bar. Catch bar at arm's length before knees bend lower than 90°. As soon as barbell is caught on locked out arms in partial squat position, stand up with barbell overhead immediately so thighs ride no lower than parallel to floor.
comments: The Power Snatch is one coordinated, continuous movement executed with speed. The grip should be wide enough to allow for partial squat with bar at arms length overhead. Hook grip is used by advanced lifters to maintain grip during snatch. Also see:

title: Sots Press
preparation: Position barbell on back of shoulders from rack or clean barbell from floor with overhand grip and push press bar to back of shoulders. Position feet shoulder width or slightly wider with feet angled outward. Place hands far apart using snatch width grip. Press bar overhead. With bar overhead, squat down in deep squat position with knees pointing outward, same angle as feet.
execution: With back straight, press barbell over head until arm are straight. Return to back of shoulders and repeat.
comments: Adequate hip flexibility is required for this mobility exercise. Spine should remain taut while maintaining deep squat position. Deep squat position requires hip crease to be below knees. Exercise can also begin or end with barbell on back of shoulders although it is typically initiated and terminated with barbell overhead.

title: 90° Jump Turns
preparation: Stand with feet shoulder width apart or slightly wider. Bend knees and hips slightly.
execution: Jump and turn 90°. Land in athletic position facing side direction. Immediately jump and turn 90° in opposite direction returning to starting position.
comments: Jump facing from front to side as fast as possible. Maintain athletic position during each land. Arms movement can assist in jumps.

title: Alternating Quick Feet
preparation: Stand perpendicular to line with toes of one foot behind line and toes of other foot in front of line. Bend arms to sides.
execution: Hop up just high enough to switch feet to opposite position and land on balls of feet. Immediately repeat alternating foot placements ahead and behind line as quickly as possible. Move bent arms forward and backward in opposition to leg position.
comments: If unfamiliar with drill, begin at slower pace and build up speed. Intensity is increased by alternating foot placement more rapidly.

title: Ankle Hop
preparation: Stand with hands on waist and feet hip-width apart.
execution: Jump up by rapidly extending ankles. Land on forefeet and Immediately jump up again pushing off primarily using feet with only minimal push-off through knees and hips.
comments: Avoid contacting ground or floor with heels.

title: Box March (high)
preparation: Stand in front of a box or platform. Place one foot on edge of box.
execution: Jump and land with opposite foot on edge of box and other foot on floor. Immediately repeat.
comments: Move feet from floor and edge of box as fast as possible. Alternate arm position with steps. Also see Box March on low platform.

title: Box March (low)
preparation: Stand in front of a short box or platform. Place one foot on edge of box.
execution: Jump and land with opposite foot on edge of box and other foot on floor. Immediately repeat.
comments: Move feet from floor and edge of box as fast as possible. Alternate arm position with steps. Also see Box March on high platform.

title: 180° Jump Turns
preparation: Stand with feet shoulder width apart or slightly wider. Squat down slightly by bending knees and hips.
execution: Jump up while turning body and throwing arms up in direction of turn. Turn 180° in air. Land in athletic position facing opposite direction. Immediately jump and turn 180° in opposite direction returning to starting position.
comments: Maintain athletic position during each land. Arms movement can assist in jumps.

title: Box Jump March
preparation: Stand in front of a short box or platform. Place one foot on edge of box.
execution: Jump up high and land with opposite foot on edge of box and other foot on floor. Immediately repeat.
comments: None.

title: Split Jump
preparation: Stand with one foot forward and other foot back with knees initially bent only slightly. Dip body down by bending legs more.
execution: Immediately jump upward. Quickly reposition legs and land with feet in opposite positions. Immediately dip body down by bending legs just short of rear knee making contact with floor. Keep torso upright and hip of rear leg straight. Repeat sequence with opposite leg movement. Continue jumps by while alternating leg positions.
comments: Flexible hip flexors are required so hip of rear leg can extend back without pulling upper body forward.

title: Squat Jump
preparation: Stand with moderately wide stance with feet pointing out slightly. Place wrists together and position bent arms in front of body.
execution: Squat down with knees angled outward slightly, same direction as feet. Allow bent arms to be lowered between knees. Immediately jump off ground as high as possible pushing off with forefeet while extending arms overhead. Land and lower body back into squat position. Repeat.
comments: Keep knees pointed same direction as feet.

title: 360° Jump Turn
preparation: Stand with feet shoulder width apart or slightly wider. Squat down by bending knees and hips. Pull arms back.
execution: Jump up while turning body and throwing arms up and in direction of turn. As body travels upward, pull arm away from direction of turn toward other shoulder. Temporarily cross legs and turn 360° through air. Land in squat position with arms forward and stand. Repeat movment in opposite direction.
comments: Alternative method is to squat down positioning bent right arm under shoulders while left arm is pulled back. Pull left arm forward and right arm back while jumping up. Tuck bent arms toward waist while turning clockwise 360° through air. Alternatively, 360° Jump Turns can be performed with successive jumps.

title: Barrier Speed Hop
preparation: Stand facing series of barriers spaced equal distance apart. Lean forward and bend arms to sides.
execution: Hop over first barrier using both feet. Land on forefeet, allowing heel to make contact or come near ground. Immediately hop over each successive barrier.
comments: Jump using both legs and forefeet to absorb impact and push-off quickly between each jump. Intensity can be increased by hoping faster, increase height of barriers, or spacing barriers further apart. Also known as Double Leg Barrier Speed Hops.

title: Box Jumps
preparation: Stand in front of box or platform with feet about shoulder width apart. Raise arms to initiate arm swing.
execution: Swing arms back behind sides while squatting down partially. Rapidly swing arms forward and jump onto platform with knees bent. Immediately jump back down to same position and repeat.
comments: To increase intensity, jump up and down faster or progressively increase platform height. Spinal Flexion will occur when jumping to very high heights. However, Chu & Myer (2014) suggest box heights of only 6' to 12" (15 to 30 cm) with top surface no less than 24" (155 cm) square.

title: Double Leg Butt Kicks
preparation: Stand with feet shoulder width apart.
execution: Squat down slightly with feet flat on floor by bending knees and hips. Jump up and kick back of heels toward buttocks. Land on both forefeet and repeat.
comments: Attempt to increase speed of jumps, keeping landings short. Any hip flexion is largely passive due to Passive Insufficiency of Rectus Femoris during knee flexion while attempting to keep hips extended.

title: Medicine Ball Split Jump
preparation: Stand with one foot forward and other foot back with knees initially bent only slightly. Hold medicine ball with both hands near chest. Dip body down by bending legs more.
execution: Jump up while extending ball upward. Quickly reposition legs, and land with feet in opposite positions. Immediately pull ball back to chest while dipping body down by bending legs just short of rear knee making contact with floor. Keep torso upright and hip of rear leg straight. Repeat sequence with opposite leg movement. Continue jumps by while alternating leg positions.
comments: Flexible hip flexors are required, so hip of rear leg can extend back without pulling upper body forward.

title: Medicine Ball Squat Jump
preparation: Stand with moderately wide stance with feet pointing out slightly. Hold medicine ball near chest with elbows pointing downward.
execution: Squat down with knees angled outward slightly, same direction as feet. Allow bent arms to be lowered between knees. Immediately jump off ground as high as possible pushing off with forefeet. Land and repeat.
comments: Keep ball near chest and keep knees pointed same direction as feet.

title: Vertical Jumps
preparation: Stand under vertical jump flag.
execution: Squat down and jump up as high as possible. Reach up with one or both hands and hit flag. Upon landing, immediately jump up again and attempt to reach higher.
comments: Use both upper and lower body movement. Keep landings short. Perform multiple jumps in succession.

title: 360° Jump Turns
preparation: Stand with feet shoulder width apart or slightly wider. Squat down by bending knees and hips. Pull arms back.
execution: Jump up while turning body and throwing arms up and in direction of turn. As body travels upward, pull arm away from direction of turn toward other shoulder. Temporarily cross legs and turn 360° through air. Land in squat position with arms forward and stand. Immediately repeat movement for at least two consecutive repetitions. Continue movement in succession in opposite direction.
comments: Alternative method is to squat down positioning bent right arm under shoulders while left arm is pulled back. Pull left arm forward and right arm back while jumping up. Tuck bent arms toward waist while turning clockwise 360° through air. Movement may also be performed by alternating directions each consecutive jump or single 360° Jump Turn allowing for recovery between jumps.

title: Box Jump
preparation: Stand in front of a secured box or platform.
comments: Rest between each jump is typically provided in effort to maximize jump height. However, jumps can be performed for reps with rests as long as it takes to step down off of box or platform and to get in starting position. Also see Box Jumps.

title: Box Squat Box Jump
preparation: Stand with shoulder width stance facing elevated platform with squat box positioned behind.
execution: Squat down with arms extended forward while attempting to push rear end back as far as possible. Sit back on squat box and rock back until torso is positioned upright. Rock body forward, swing arms forward, and immediately jump off ground onto elevated platform. Land feet on top of elevated platform.
comments: Try to keep knees from traveling forward while reaching rear end back onto squat box. Ideally, knees could be behind ankles (further back than what is shown). In this position, greater stretch reflex can be created through hips (via hamstrings) once torso rocks forward just before jump. However, other hip extensors can engage in stretch-shortening cycle in lieu of hamstrings' optimized involvement.

title: Kneeling Squat Jump
preparation: Kneel on floor with knee pads, feet extended behind body. Begin upright with hips extended.
execution: Swing arms and hips back. Quickly swing arm forward. As arms continue to travel upward forcefully, extend hips so knees and feet along entire body is thrown upward. Then swiftly pull legs forward and land on feet with arms in front of body. Kneel back down on floor and repeat.
comments: Knee pads protect skin from abrasions.

title: Hurdle Depth Jump
preparation: Stand on plyo box positioned in front of hurdle.
execution: Step forward off edge of plyo box. Pull both arms back and drop to ground. Land on both feet, throw both arms forward, and jump up over hurdle. Pull legs up to clear hurdle. Extend legs forward and down. Land on both feet, bend legs to absorb impact and stand.
comments: Spine is kept straight during jump push off. However spine flexes when legs are pulled up. Spine also extends during final landing. Avoid pulling legs out to sides by rotating hips in effort to clear legs over hurdle.

title: Hurdle Jump
preparation: Stand facing collapsible hurdle.
execution: Squat down while swinging both arms back behind body to each side. Swing arms forward and upward. Jump over the hurdle by extending hips, knees, and feet. Pull legs up over hurdle by flexing hips and knees while reaching arms forward. When feet are over hurdle extend legs forward and pull arms down toward sides. Land on both legs, squat down to absorb impact, and stand upright.
comments: Hurdle should be collapsible in case it is not cleared. Jump requires static spinal extension as body initially extends and during landing. However, spinal flexion occurs as legs are pulled upward, following complete hip flexion in effort to raise legs to maximal height. Consequently, dynamic spinal extension is observed when hips and body are re-extended.

title: Hurdle Jumps
preparation: Stand facing collapsible hurdles or barriers (12" to 36", 30 to 90 cm) set up in a row.
execution: Squat down and jump over hurdle with feet together using a double arm swing. Upon landing, immediately jump over next hurdle.
comments: Use both upper and lower body movements. Keep landings short. Hurdles should be collapsible in case they are not cleared. Space hurdles or barriers according to ability. Increase height of barrier as ability improves. Perform 2 or more jumps in succession. Jumps require static spinal extension as body initially extends and during landing. However, spinal flexion occurs as legs are pulled upward, following complete hip flexion in effort to raise legs to maximal height. Consequently, dynamic spinal extension is observed when hips and body are re-extended.

title: Seated Box Jump
preparation: Sit on squat box facing elevated platform. Position lower legs vertical or ankles slightly forward of knees. Place arms down to sides.
execution: Swing arms forward. On arm's back swing, begin to lean torso forward. Swing arms forward again, shift body weight onto feet, and jump off ground onto elevated platform. Land feet on top of elevated platform.
comments: Ideally, knees could be behind ankles (further back than what is shown) when initially seated. In this position, greater stretch reflex can be created through hips (via hamstrings) once torso rocks forward, just before jump. However, other hip extensors can engage in stretch-shortening cycle in lieu of hamstrings' optimized involvement. A plyo-platform, bench, stool, or chair can be used. Increase height of elevated platform as ability improves. Plyometric boxes or stacked mats may be used as elevated platform. Also see similar Box Squat Jump.

title: Single Leg Vertical Hop
preparation: Stand on one leg; lift other foot off of floor by bending knee.
execution: Quickly squat down by bending knee and hip while extending raised leg back and swinging arms back. Immediately jump upward while swinging raised leg and arms forward and upward. Extend body vertically. Land on either same foot or both feet. Continue jumps for desired number of repetitions then repeat on other leg.
comments: Land on forefoot then come down on whole foot. Either repeat for continuous hops or rest longer between jump for more maximal effort. Alternatively, consider alternating between legs.

title: Single Leg Vertical Jump
preparation: Stand on one leg by elevating other leg behind body with knee bent.
execution: Quickly squat down by bending knee and hip while extending raised leg back and swinging arms back. Immediately jump upward while swinging raised leg and arms forward and upward. Extend body vertically. Lower arms down to sides and land on either same foot or both feet. Bend ankles, knees, and hips to absorb impact. Repeat movement pattern on opposite leg.
comments: Use both upper and lower body movement. Land on forefoot then come down on whole foot.

title: Stair Jumps
preparation: Stand facing bottom of staircase.
execution: Squat down and jump up steps using double arm swing. Upon landing, immediately jump up steps again.
comments: Use both upper and lower body movements. Keep landings short. Increase number of steps jumped each repetition as ability improves. Perform 2 or more jumps in succession.

title: Tuck Jump
preparation: Stand straight with feet shoulder width apart.
execution: Swinging both arms back to each side and squat down by bending knees and hips. Swing arms forward and jump upward. Immediately pull knees up toward chest. Extend body vertically, land, and squat down to absorb impact. Pull arms back and repeat if needed.
comments: Movement can be performed in successive repetitions or as single rep. If performed as successive reps, immediately jump upward after making contact with floor.

title: Vertical Power Jump
preparation: Stand under vertical jump flag.
execution: Quickly swing both arms up, then immediately down. Bend over and squat while pulling both arms back behind body to each side. Immediately swing arms forward and upward while raising torso upright and heels slightly off of floor. Raise arms upward while extending hips, knees, and feet. Touch highest flag with one hand. Land on both feet and squat down. Drop arms and stand upright.
comments: Use both upper and lower body movement. Dynamic spinal extension may occur in some individuals performing maximal attempts. Slight spine flexion may occur during bent over squat position dependent upon participants depth and flexibility. Also spinal hyper-extension may occur as feet leave ground with body extended.

title: Vertical Jump (no reach)
preparation: Stand near wall with measurement chart.
execution: Quickly swing both arms up, then immediately down. Bend over and squat while pulling both arms back behind body to each side. Immediately swing arms forward and upward while raising torso upright and heels slightly off of floor. Raise arms upward while extending hips, knees, and feet. As body continues to rise and fall, continue to rotate both arms in circular pattern down and behind then up and forward. Land on both feet and squat down with arms extended forward. Stand upright.
comments: Since jump height can be measured from floor to bottom of feet, legs should be kept extended with feet positioned horizontally at height of jump.

title: Box Depth Jump
preparation: Stand on plyo box close to front edge facing platform.
execution: Step off box landing on both feet between box and platform. Jump off the ground as fast as possible onto the platform.
comments: Increase height of platform as ability improves. Plyometric boxes or stacked mats may be used.

title: Multiple Box Depth Jumps
preparation: Position plyo boxes in series spaced apart with room to jump between each box. Stand in front of first box with narrower than shoulder width stance.
execution: Jump to first box landing with both feet, drop to floor then immediately jump to next box. Repeat until last box is reached.
comments: Plyo boxes can be of same height or of progressive heights arranged from lowest to highest. Greater box heights decrease ground reaction time. Unless box heights are 12" (30 cm) or less, step off of edges of each box instead of jumping up and off of boxes. If boxes are deep, land on box, then step to far end of box before stepping off. Spine is kept straight during jump push off and jumping up to short boxes. However spine flexes when legs are pulled up onto taller boxes. Consequently, spine also extends just before final landing.

title: Multiple Box Jumps (Progressive)
preparation: Position progressively higher plyo boxes in series spaced apart with room to jump between each box. Stand in front of first box with narrower than shoulder width stance.
execution: Jump to first box landing with both feet, drop to floor then immediately jump to next box. Repeat jumping from floor to next box until last box is reached.

title: Barbell Guillotine Bench Press
preparation: Lie supine on bench. Dismount barbell from rack over upper chest using wide oblique overhand grip.
execution: Lower weight to neck to upper chest. Upper arms will be perpendicular to torso. Press bar until arms are extended. Repeat.
comments: Upper arm travels in perpendicular path to torso to minimize anterior deltoid involvement and mimics upper arm positioning of Chest Flies. The oblique grip (hand positioned with palm diagonal on bar) allows elbows to be more easily turned outward to each side. Also see Bench Press Analysis.

title: Barbell Bench Press (power lift)
preparation: Lie supine on bench. Grasp bar with overhand and slightly wider than shoulder width grip. Arch back, extend hips, and position feet back flat on floor. Dismount barbell from rack over chest.
execution: Lower weight to lower chest. Press bar upward until arms are extended. Repeat.
comments: Competitive lifting requires bar to make contact with chest above bottom of the sternum with momentary pause. Gluteus Maximus, Erector Spinae, Quadriceps, and other muscles may be considered significant stabilizers during actual powerlift.

title: Barbell Board Press
preparation: Lie supine on bench. Grasp bar with overhand and slightly wider than shoulder width grip. Arch back, extend hips, and position feet back flat on floor. Dismount barbell from rack over chest. Have assistant hold board on center of chest.
execution: Lower weight toward lower chest on   to board. Press bar upward until arms are extended. Repeat.
comments: Instead of assistant holding bench board, it can be strapped to torso, as shown. Boards of varying thicknesses (1, 2, or 3) can be used. Board Press develops strength in middle range of motion for Bench Press. Gluteus Maximus, Erector Spinae, Quadriceps, and other muscles may be considered significant stabilizers during actual powerlift.

title: Barbell Floor Press
preparation: Lie supine on floor at base of power rack. Place feet on floor with knees bent. Grasp bar from rack with overhand and slightly wider than shoulder width grip. Dismount barbell from rack over chest.
execution: Lower weight to lower chest until the triceps are completely on the floor. Relax momentarily, then press bar upward until arms are extended. Repeat.
comments: Floor Presses are also said to build explosive strength by breaking eccentric/concentric chain (AKA: stretch-shortening cycle) normally activated at bottom of standard Bench Press.

title: Barbell Bench Press (with chains)
preparation: Lie supine on bench. Grasp barbell loaded with thick chains from each side, with overhand and slightly wider than shoulder width grip. Dismount barbell from rack over chest.
execution: Lower weight to chest. Press bar upward until arms are extended. Repeat.
comments: Chains provide increasing resistance as barbell moves away from floor. Chains can be used during either Powerlift-style Bench Press or Standard-style Bench Press. Also see Bench Press Analysis.

title: Barbell Decline Bench Press
preparation: Lie supine on decline bench with feet under leg brace. Dismount barbell from rack over chest using wide oblique overhand grip.
execution: Lower weight to chest. Press bar until arms are extended. Repeat.
comments: Range of motion will be compromised if grip is too wide. Latissimus Dorsi involvement is l   ow on Decline Bench Press (Barnett 1995). Also see Bench Press Analysis.

title: Buffalo Bar Decline Bench Press
preparation: With middle of bar arching upward, lie supine on decline bench with feet under leg brace. Dismount barbell from rack over chest using wide oblique overhand grip.
execution: Lower weight to chest. Press bar until arms are extended. Repeat.
comments: Buffalo bar offers slightly greater range of motion compared to Barbell Decline Bench Press. Range of motion will be compromised if grip is too wide. Latissimus Dorsi involvement is l   ow on Decline Bench Press (Barnett 1995). Also see Bench Press Analysis.

title: Cambered Bar Decline Bench Press
preparation: With camber arching upward, lie supine on decline bench with feet under leg brace. Dismount barbell from rack over chest using wide oblique overhand grip.
comments: Cambered bar offers greater range of motion compared to Decline Bench Press. Range of motion will be compromised if grip is too wide. Latissimus Dorsi involvement is low on Decline Bench Press (Barnett 1995). Also see Bench Press Analysis.

title: Cable Chest Dip
preparation: Step between wide dip bars with dip belt around waist. Kneel as close as possible to low pulley and attach cable to dip belt. Stand up and mount dip bar with oblique grip (bar diagonal under palm), arms straight with shoulders above hands. Keep hips and knees bent.
execution: Lower body by bending arms, allowing elbows to flare out to sides. When slight stretch is felt in chest or shoulders, push body up until arms are straight and repeat.
comments: The animation depicts dip bar placed in front of low pull cable. If cable dip machine is available, attach hip harness and step up to dip bar. Also see Cable Triceps Dip.

title: Cable Isolateral Decline Fly
preparation: Grasp two opposing high pulley dumbbell attachments. Lie supine on decline bench, in middle and perpendicular to both pulleys. Slightly bend elbows and internally rotate shoulders so elbows are back.
execution: Bring cable stirrups together above upper abdomen in hugging motion; elbows in fixed position and shoulders internally rotated so elbows are to sides. Return to starting position until chest is slightly stretched. Repeat.
comments: Since incline of bench does not actually affect angle of pull as with dumbbells, similar effect can be achieved on flat bench if cable stirrups are brought together above upper abdomen instead of chest.   Keep shoulders internally rotated so elbows point downward at bottom position and outward at top position. Keep elbows at fixed angle, only slightly bent. Anterior Deltoid will no longer act as synergist in steep decline bench position.

title: Cable Isolateral Lying Fly
preparation: Grasp two opposing low pulley stirrup attachments. Lie supine on bench, in middle and perpendicular to both pulleys. Slightly bend elbows and internally rotate shoulders so elbows are back.
execution: Bring cable attachments together in hugging motion with elbows in fixed position and shoulders internally rotated so elbows are to sides. Return to starting position until slight stretch. Repeat.
comments: Keep shoulders internally rotated so elbows point downward at bottom position and outward at top position. Keep elbows at fixed angle, only slightly bent.

title: Cable Isolateral Cable Fly
preparation: Sit on seat and grasp stirrups to each side. Slightly bend elbows and internally rotate shoulders so elbows are back.
execution: Keeping elbows pointed high, bring cable attachments together in hugging motion with elbows in fixed position. Return to starting position until slight stretch. Repeat.
comments: Shoulders are kept internally rotated so elbows are pointing back   and out to sides.

title: Cable Isolateral Standing Fly
preparation: Grasp two opposing high pulley dumbbell attachments. Stand with pulleys to each side. Bend over slightly by flexing hips and knees. Bend elbows slightly and internally rotate shoulders so elbows are back initially.
execution: Bring cable attachments together in hugging motion with elbows in fixed position. Keep shoulders internally rotated so elbows are pointed upward at top and out to sides at bottom. Return to starting position until chest muscles are stretched. Repeat.
comments: Under greater resistance, positioning torso at lower angle downward (by bending over at hips), will allow upper body weight to counterbalance upward pull of cables, as opposed to stepping forward and struggling with   backward pull of cables.

title: Cable Bar Bench Press
preparation: Position adjustable pulley higher than intended height but within arms reach from bench. Position bench under cable bar. Lie supine on bench. While lying on bench, reach over and lower each pulley into intended position so that cables are taut in lowest position with bar on chest. Grasp bar with both hands using wide oblique overhand grip.
execution: Press bar upward until arms are extended. Lower bar to chest or until slight stretch is felt in shoulders. Repeat.
comments: Range of motion will be compromised if grip is too wide. Also see Bench Press Analysis and Cable Bar Standing Bench Press.

title: Cable Isolateral Bench Press
preparation: Lie on bench and grasp stirrups attached to low cable pulley on each side. Position stirrups out to each side of chest with bent arm under each wrist.
execution: Push stirrups up over each shoulder until arms are straight and parallel to one another. Return stirrups to original position, until slight stretch is felt in shoulders or chest. Repeat.
comments: Stirrup should follow slight arch pattern, above upper arm between elbow and chest at bottom, traveling inward over each shoulder at t   op. Also see Bench Press Analysis.

title: Single Leg Box Depth Jump
preparation: Stand on short plyo box or platform. Stand on one leg with other leg elevated or extended forward.
execution: Hop forward off box or platform on one foot. Pull arms back to each side and land on one foot. Immediately throw arms forward and jump up to higher box or platform. Land again on same foot, bending leg to absorb impact. Repeat with other leg.
comments: Land on forefoot then come down on whole foot. Alternative method is to begin on both feet, step off and jump with one foot and land on both feet.

title: Single Leg Hurdle Depth Jump
preparation: Stand on plyo box positioned in front of hurdle.
comments: Spine is kept straight during jump push off. However spine may flex when legs are pulled up if hurdle is high. Spine also extends during final landing. Avoid pulling legs out to sides by rotating hips in effort to clear legs over hurdle.

title: Single Leg Vertical Depth Jump
preparation: Stand on plyo box. Stand on one leg with other leg elevated or extended forward slightly.
execution: Hop forward off box on one foot. Pull arms back to each side and land on one foot. Immediately throw arms forward and jump up. Land again on same foot, bending leg to absorb impact. Repeat with other leg.
comments: Land on forefoot. Alternative method is to begin on both feet, step off and jump with one foot and land on both feet.

title: Single Leg Vertical Depth Power Jump
preparation: Stand on short plyo box or platform. Stand on one leg with other leg elevated or extended forward.

title: Single Leg Vertical Power Jump
preparation: Stand on one leg under vertical jump flag.
execution: Quickly squat down by bending knee and hip while extending raised leg back and swinging arms back. Immediately jump upward while swinging raised leg and arms forward and upward. Lower arms down to sides and land on either same foot or both feet. Bend ankles, knees, and hips to absorb impact. Repeat movement pattern on opposite leg.
comments: Use both upper and lower body movement. Land on forefoot then come down on whole foot.

title: Staircase Depth Jump
preparation: Stand on box close to front edge facing staircase.
execution: Step off box landing on both feet between box and staircase. Jump off the ground as fast as possible to highest step.
comments: None.

title: Vertical Depth Jump
preparation: Stand on box close to front edge facing vertical jump flag.
execution: Step off box landing on both feet under vertical jump flag. Jump off the ground as fast as possible. Reach up with one or both hands and hit the highest flag possible.
comments: Dynamic spinal extension may occur in some individuals primarily due to spinal hyper-extension which may occur when body extends and feet leave ground.

title: Line Hop
preparation: Stand behind line with arms bent to sides.
execution: Hop quickly to front of line and land on balls of feet. Immediately hop back behind line. Continue hopping forward and backward as quickly as possible while moving bent arms forward and backward in unison with each hop to maintain balance and momentum.
comments: If unfamiliar with drill, begin at slower pace and build up speed. Intensity is increased by hopping forward and backward more rapidly. If drifting occurs, continue rapid tempo while attempting to correct positioning, gradually if required.

title: Speed Hop
preparation: Stand with both feet together. Lean forward and bend arms to sides.
execution: Hop forward using both feet. Repeat until desired distance is reached.
comments: None

title: Cone Jumps
preparation: Stand facing series of cones spaced equally apart in straight line. Lean forward and bend arms to sides.
execution: Jump forward over first cone using both feet. Land on forefeet, allowing heel to make contact or come near ground. Immediately hop over each cone in succession, pushing off and landing on forefeet.
comments: Jump using both legs and forefeet to absorb impact and push-off quickly between each jump. Intensity can be increased by hoping faster, increase height of cones, or spacing cones further apart. Also known as Double Leg Cone Hops.

title: Alternate Bound (with double arm action)
preparation: Begin by jogging to initialize momentum.
execution: Bring right foot forward while pushing off with left foot. As right thigh travels upward parallel to ground, forcefully bring both arms forward to help propel body forward (aka: gather forward). As left leg comes through, extend right leg back and keep it extended for duration of push-off. Hold this extended stride for short time while quickly pulling both arms behind body. Land left foot and drive right leg forward while bringing both arms forward. Extend left leg back and bring arms back. Continued bounding for length or duration of the drill.
comments: This drill is somewhat of exaggerated running drill. Bound forward into air as high as possible by emphasizing high knee lifts. Focus on pushing against ground from landing through takeoff attempting to maintain hang time. Each stride should have an aesthetic glide or float pattern appearing effortless yet demonstrate power during take off.

title: Alternate Bound (with single arm action)
preparation: Begin by jogging to initialize momentum.
execution: Push off with right foot and reach forward with left arm while bring right leg forward with knee bent and thigh parallel to ground. Extend right leg back and keep it extended for duration of push-off. Hold this extended stride for short time and land left foot. Drive right leg forward to front bent position and extend left leg back while left arm reaches forward in same manner. Continued bounding for length or duration of the drill.
comments: Drill is exaggerated running movement. Bound forward into air as high as possible by emphasizing high knee lifts. Focus on pushing against ground from landing through takeoff attempting to maintain hang time. Each stride should have an aesthetic glide or float pattern appearing effortless yet demonstrate power during take off. Attempt to either maintain speed or cover as much distance as possible by making each stride long. As heel makes contact with ground, it absorbs impact by eccentric contraction of muscle responsible for dorsi flexion of ankle slightly slowing speed at which remainder of foot hits ground.

title: Broad Jump
preparation: Stand behind line with feet shoulder width apart. Squat down partially and raise arms forward.
execution: Swing arms back behind sides and lean forward. Rapidly swing arms forward and jump forward as far as possible with both feet close together. Land with both feet together with legs bend and stand up.
comments: Avoid falling backwards and maintain position of feet when landing.

title: Butt Kick Jumps
preparation: Stand behind starting line.
execution: Squat down and jump forward as far as possible using double arm swing. In air, pull heels toward buttocks and immediately extend legs for landing. Upon landing, immediately jump forward again using same movement pattern.
comments: Use both upper and lower body movements. Keep landings short. Perform two or more jumps in succession.

title: Broad Jumps
preparation: Stand behind starting line.
execution: Squat down and jump forward as far as possible using double arm swing. Upon landing, immediately jump forward again.
comments: Use both upper and lower body movements. Keep landings short. Perform 2 or more jumps in succession.

title: Single Leg Line Hop
preparation: Stand behind line with arms bent to sides. Lift foot off of floor by bending knee. Slightly bend hip and knee of supporting leg.
execution: Hop quickly to front of line and land on forefoot. Immediately hop back behind line. Continue hopping forward and backward as quickly as possible while keeping foot elevated. Move bent arms forward and backward in unison with each hop to maintain balance and momentum.
comments: If unfamiliar with drill, begin at slower pace and build up speed. Intensity is increased by hopping forward and backward more rapidly. If drifting occurs, continue rapid tempo while attempting to correct positioning, gradually if required.

title: Double Leg Bound
preparation: Squat partially down and pull arms back behind body to each side.
execution: Swing arms forward and jump high and far. Immediately pull heels to buttocks. Begin to pull arms back just before descent. Cycle into high knee position. Land with feet slightly in front of body and arms behind body to each side. Absorb landing by lowering body to partial squat position. Repeat bounding.
comments: Drill emphasizes optimal lower body running mechanics while incorporating additional ground reaction force. Perform for maximum height, maximum distance, or combination of both. Also known as Double Leg Cycle Hops.

title: Single Leg Hops
preparation: Stand on one leg with other leg extended back. Squat partially down and pull arms back behind body to each side.
execution: Swing arms forward, kick elevated leg forward, and jump forward. Begin to pull arms back just before descent. Land with arms behind body to each side. Absorb landing by lowering body to partial squat position. Continue forward jumps until desired distance is reached. Repeat on other foot.
comments: Land on forefoot then come down on whole foot. Arms can be pulled behind body either through sagittal plane, transverse planes, or combination of both directions.

title: Standing Triple Jump
preparation: Stand with both feet behind starting line or with feet staggered, first foot behind starting line and second foot back.
execution: Hop forward on first leg and land on same foot while keeping second foot elevated off of floor. Hop forward again on first foot and land on second foot. Hop forward on second foot and land on both feet.
comments: Crouching or rocking leading up to jump is permitted. Avoid stepping or falling back after landing. Triple jump mat can also be used. Measurement can be taken from take off line from back of heel.

title: Long Depth Jump
preparation: Stand on box close to front edge facing measured floor.
execution: Step off box landing on both feet behind starting line. Jump off the ground as fast and as far as possible.
comments: None.

title: Single Leg Speed Hops
preparation: Stand on right leg; lift left foot off of floor by bending knee.
execution: Similar to initiating running movement, lean forward, bend supporting right leg, swing bent left forward, pull left arm back, and bend right arm forward. Instead 'run' on only one leg by hopping forward on right foot. As traveling through air, pull right leg forward and bend left leg back while swinging left arm forward and right arm back. Right forefoot makes contact with floor. As entire foot lands on floor, pull left arm back and right arm forward. Continue hopping forward on right leg using same single-leg 'running' pattern until desired distance is reached. Repeat on other right foot.
comments: Initial hop may or may not resemble Single-leg Hops with single concurrent bilateral arm motion. Land on forefoot then come down on whole foot.

title: Triple Jump
preparation: Stand 17 to 18 strides from block. Run to block.
execution: Hop forward on first leg and land on same foot while keeping second foot elevated off of floor. Hop forward again on first foot and land on second foot. Jump forward on second foot. Bring both feet together with knees parallel to chest while jumping into the pit. Land jump with heels first.
comments: Do not run beyond board during hop. While in air during hop, skip, and jump, throw arms in front of body, no lower than chest and no higher than chin height. See jump in slow motion.

title: Lateral Box Shuffle
preparation: Stand with one foot on box and other foot off, down by side of box.
execution: Jump sideways so opposite foot is on box and the other is off by side of box. Repeat shuffle side to side.
comments: Upper body motion can be emphasized by throwing arms up during leaps. Attempt to increase speed of jumps, keeping landings short. Perform multiple jumps in succession.

title: Lateral Bosu Shuffle
preparation: Stand with one foot on box and other foot off, down by side of box.
execution: Jump sideways so opposite foot is on box and the other is off by side of box. Repeat shuffle side to side.
comments: Upper body motion can be emphasized by throwing arms ups during leaps. See example. Attempt to increase speed of jumps, keeping landings short. Perform multiple jumps in succession.

title: Lateral Bound
preparation: Stand with feet close together. Bend knees and hips slightly with arms bent to sides or to front. Lift right foot up, slightly off of floor.
execution: Jump far to right side and land right foot with left foot off of ground. Jump far to left side landing on right foot. Continue to bound from side to side.
comments: Attempt to progressively increase speed, length, height, or duration of jumps depending on training goal. Hip adductors are activated both dynamically (during bound) and isometrically (during landing on single leg). Also see Medicine Ball Lateral Bound.

title: Lateral Line Hop
preparation: Stand with feet together to one side of line on floor. Bend hips and knees sightly with arms to sides.
execution: Jump to opposite side of line and land on forefeet. Continue jumping from side to side landing on forefeet.
comments: Hop side to side of line as quick as possible. Push off of forefeet with feet together.

title: Quadrant Jumps (diagonal)
preparation: Stand with both feet together in one of two lower sections of quadrant.
execution: Jump forward into upper quadrant then immediately jump diagonally back. Immediately jump forward to other upper quadrant, then diagonally back to starting square. Immediately repeat diagonally crossing pattern sequence.
comments: Perform this exercise as fast as possible while avoiding stepping on lines and maintaining balance and control. Jump and land on forefeet with heels slightly elevated off of floor.

title: Quadrant Jumps (square)
preparation: Stand with both feet together in one of two lower sections of quadrant.
execution: Jump to each square of quadrant in clockwise direction.
comments: Perform this exercise as fast as possible while avoiding stepping on lines and maintaining balance and control. Jump and land on forefeet with heels slightly elevated off of floor.

title: Slalom Jump
preparation: Stand with both feet together to one side of extended line.
execution: Jump on both forefeetfeet in alternating diagonal pattern across each side of line. Continue jumping in zigzag pattern over line moving forward until marked distance is reached or for set time.
comments: Suggested length of line is approximately 10-15 feet (3-4.5 m) long, but it can be any length. Attempt to increase speed of jumps, keeping landings short. Perform multiple jumps in succession. Also see exercise performed with Agility Ladder.

title: Slalom Jump (with agility ladder)
preparation: Place Agility Ladder on floor. Stand with your right foot in first box and with left foot outside box.
execution: Jump up with both feet to right and forward direction and land with left foot in box. Immediately push off both feet forward towards left direction to next box and land with right foot in box.
comments: Attempt to increase speed of jumps, keeping landings short. Perform multiple jumps in succession. Movement can also be performed without Agility Ladder.

title: Lateral Barrier Jumps
preparation: Stand with side to barrier.
execution: Jump sideways to opposite side of barrier. Upon landing, immediately jump to other side.
comments: Use both upper and lower body movements. Attempt to increase speed of jumps, keeping landings short. Perform multiple jumps in succession.

title: Lateral Box Jumps
preparation: Stand on box.
execution: Jump sideways down to side of box. Jump back onto box, then jump down to the other side of the box. Jump back onto the box and repeat.
comments: Upper body motion can be emphasized by throwing arms ups during leaps. Attempt to increase speed of jumps, keeping landings short. Perform multiple jumps in succession.

title: Lateral Cone Hop
preparation: Stand with cone to one side.
execution: Jump sideways to opposite side of cone. Upon landing, immediately jump to other side.
comments: Use both upper and lower body movements. Attempt to increase speed of jumps, keeping landings short. Perform multiple jumps in succession.

title: Lateral Cone Jumps
preparation: Line up series of cones in straight line. Stand at one end of cone facing sideways.
execution: Jump laterally over first cone, land on both feet between first and second cone, then immediately jump laterally off the ground over the next cone. Repeat until last cone is reached, perform in opposite direction until jumping laterally over first cone in starting position.

title: Long Slalom Jumps
preparation: Stand with both feet together to outside of one of two sets of very short hurdles position in two staggered parallel columns.
execution: Jump with both feet in alternating diagonal (zigzag) pattern across first hurdle and land on forefeet across to opposite side of hurdle in other column. Continue to land and jump on both forefeet to outside of each short hurdle alternating to adjacent hurdle in opposite row until last hurdle had been reached.
comments: Take off and land with both forefeet. Keeping landings short. Adjust both arms to gain additional momentum and balance.

title: Medicine Ball Lateral Bound
preparation: Stand with feet close together, holding medicine ball with hands on each side of ball. Bend knees and hips slightly with arms bent to sides or to front. Lift right foot up slightly off of floor.
execution: Jump far to right side. Land right foot with left foot off of ground. Position ball forward, away from body. Immediately jump far to left side, landing on right foot. Again, position ball forward away from body. Continue to bound from side to side in same fashion.
comments: Ball can also be kept nearer chest without extending arm forward on each land, in which case, shoulder articulation becomes static. Attempt to progressively increase speed, length, height, or duration of jumps depending on training goal. Hip adductors are activated both dynamically (during bound) and isometrically (during landing on single leg). Also see Lateral bound without medicine ball.

title: Single Leg Lateral Line Hop
preparation: Straddle line on floor with arms to sides. Lift foot off of floor by bending knee. Slightly bend hip and knee of supporting leg.
execution: Jump to opposite side of line and land on forefoot. Continue to jump side to side landing on forefeet while keeping foot elevated.
comments: Muscles which Abduct Hip and Laterally Flex Spine also server to stabilize these joints when jumping or standing on one leg.

title: Single Leg Lateral Hop (over barrier)
preparation: Stand on one leg to one side of cone. Slightly bend hip and knee of supporting leg.
execution: Jump over cone to opposite side, landing on forefoot. Continue to jump over cone from side to side landing on forefeet while keeping foot elevated.
comments: Muscles which Abduct Hip and Laterally Flex Spine also server to stabilize these joints when jumping or standing on one leg.

title: Single Leg Zigzag Hop
preparation: Straddle line on floor with arms to sides. Lift foot off of floor by bending knee. Slightly bend hip and knee of supporting leg.
execution: Jump on one foot in alternating diagonal (zigzag) pattern across each side of line. Jump and land on forefoot while keeping other foot elevated. Repeat with opposite foot.
comments: Attempt to increase speed of jumps, keeping landings short. Some individuals my exhibit spinal rotation during this drill, alternating from right to left swivels each jump.

title: Medicine Ball Arch Chop
preparation: Stand holding medicine ball with hands on each side of ball. Place feet wide apart, pointing approximately 45° outward and knees slightly bent. Bend over and place ball near outside of one foot.
execution: Raise ball up to side and up over head so torso is upright. Bend over to opposite side and lower ball down to outside of other foot. Repeat with opposite movement, alternating raising and lowering ball back and forth between sides.
comments: Start slow, gradually increasing speed throughout set. Perform prescribed repetitions or for time. See Spot Reduction Myth.

title: Medicine Ball Single Leg Chop
preparation: Stand holding medicine ball with hands on each side of ball. Raise ball above head with arms bent. Raise one foot up off floor.
execution: Keeping one foot off of floor, lower ball to top of foot by bending over at hip while pulling elevated leg back. Bend supporting knee slightly while reaching down and placing ball on foot. Immediately lift ball in opposite path. Stand upright while raising ball above head. Continue to raise and lower ball on one leg. Repeat by performing movement on opposite leg.
comments: Start slow, gradually increasing speed throughout set. Perform prescribed repetitions or for time. If hip (ie: hamstrings) are sufficiently flexible, spine may remain straight with no articulation (static).

title: Medicine Ball Vertical Chop
preparation: Stand holding medicine ball with hands on each side of ball. Place feet wide apart, pointing outward slightly. Raise ball above head with arms bent.
execution: Lower ball to front of body. Bend over while continuing to lower ball down and back between legs. Immediately lift ball in opposite path. Stand upright while raising ball up in front of body. Continue to raise ball up over and behind head. Immediately repeat movement.
comments: Start slow, gradually increasing speed throughout set. Perform prescribed repetitions or for time.

title: Medicine Ball Chest Throw Sit-up (on wall)
preparation: Sit on floor facing wall with knees bent. Hold medicine ball with both hands near chest.
execution: Lie back while hold ball near chest. Raise upper body up by first flexing spine and then flexing hips. While completing sit-up, throw ball toward wall. Catch ball bouncing off of wall with both hands. Reposition ball near chest while laying back. Repeat.
comments: Perform prescribed repetitions or for time. Feet may also be secured under weighted or anchored structure. If feet are not being supported by fixed object, legs may need to be extended somewhat to offer adequate counter balance to upper body.

title: Medicine Ball Chest Throw Sit-up (with partner)
preparation: Two participants face each other and sit on floor facing each other with knees bent. Participants’ feet point toward one another. First participant holds medicine ball near chest with both hands.
execution: First participant lies back while holding ball. First participant raises upper body up by first flexing spine and then flexing hips. While completing sit-up, first participant throws ball to partner. Partner catches ball with both hands and executes same sequence. Participant and parter continue to exchange ball.
comments: Perform prescribed repetitions or for time. Feet may also be secured under weighted or anchored structure. If feet are not being supported by fixed object, legs may need to be extended somewhat to offer adequate counter balance to upper body.

title: Pike Jump
preparation: Stand with feet shoulder width or narrower.
execution: Swinging both arms back to each side and squat down by bending knees and hips. Swing arms forward and jump upward. Immediately kick both legs forward while reaching forward so hands make contact with feet with legs straight. Extend body vertically, land on forefeet, and squat down to absorb impact. Pull arms back and repeat if needed.
comments: Movement can be performed in successive repetitions (see Pike Jumps) or as single rep as shown.

title: Lying Leg Raise Throwdown (with partner)
preparation: Lie supine on floor with feet together. Partner stands with feet at each side of head. Grasp partner's legs, just above ankles to each side. Raise legs vertically so partner can bend over slightly to touch feet or ankles.
execution: Partner throws legs toward floor by pushing feet or ankles outward. Immediately lift straight legs back to vertical position by flexing hips. Partner continues to throw legs to floor by pushing feet or ankles away.
comments: Continue to hold on to partners legs, just above ankles. Keep legs straight and avoid allowing heels to touch floor. Partner can bend knees in order to more easily bend over particularly if they are taller than participant. Partner can also push feet away to each side, alternating between forward and side leg raises and throws. Also see Lying Diagonal Leg Raise Throwdown.

title: Lying Diagonal Leg Raise Throwdown (with partner)
execution: Partner throws legs diagonally toward floor by pushing feet or ankles outward slightly to one side. Immediately lift straight legs back to vertical position by flexing hips. Partner continues to throw legs to floor by pushing feet or ankles away to one side.
comments: Continue to hold on to partners legs, just above ankles. Keep legs straight and avoid allowing heels to touch floor. Partner can bend knees in order to more easily bend over particularly if they are taller than participant. Partner can also push feet away to each side, alternating between forward and side leg raises and throws. Also see Lying Leg Raise Throwdown without diagonal movement.

title: Medicine Ball Overhead Throw Sit-up (with partner)
preparation: Two participants face each other and sit on floor facing each other with knees bent. Participants’ feet point toward one another. First participant holds medicine ball with both hands.
execution: First participant lies back with ball over head and taps ball to floor. First participant immediately throws ball to partner from over head while sitting up. Partner catches ball slightly above and in front of head and repeats steps. Participant and parter continue to volley ball back and forth.
comments: Perform prescribed repetitions or for time. Feet may also be secured under weighted or anchored structure. If feet are not being supported by fixed object, legs may need to be extended somewhat to offer adequate counter balance to upper body. If upper back does not come completely down at end of movement, abdominal muscles may only be isometrically involved in exercise. See Spot Reduction Myth. Also see Medicine Ball Sit-up on Machine.

title: Medicine Ball Overhead Throw Sit-up
preparation: Sit on floor facing wall with knees bent. Hold medicine ball with both hands.
execution: Lie back with ball over head and tap ball to floor. Immediately throw ball to wall from over head while sitting up. Ball rebounds off of wall. Catch ball slightly above and in front of head. Continue to volley ball against wall.
comments: Perform prescribed repetitions or for time. Wall should be impact resistant to avoid structural damage. Feet may also be secured under weighted or anchored structure. If feet are not being supported by fixed object, legs may need to be extended somewhat to offer adequate counter balance to upper body.
